title: Jhansi Fort
tagline: Jhansi Fort — heritage city of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Jhansi Fort or Jhansi ka Qila is a fortress situated on a large hilltop called Bangira, in Uttar Pradesh.
overview.description: Jhansi Fort or Jhansi ka Qila is a fortress situated on a large hilltop called Bangira, in Uttar Pradesh. It served as a stronghold of the Karhade Brahmin Kings in Balwant Nagar (old name of Jhansi) from the 11th through the 17th century. There was another British fortress called the Star Fort which was located south of the main fortress of the town, at Jhansi Cantt. It was a primary British settlement in the town and had been constructed in accordance to a treaty of the British with the Newalkar Maharaja of Jhansi in 1804, through which Jhansi became subordinate to the British.

topPlaces.name: Jhansi State
topPlaces.description: Jhansi was an independent princely state ruled by the Maratha Newalkar dynasty under suzerainty of British India from 1804 till 1853, when the British authorities took over the state under the terms of the Doctrine of Lapse, and renamed it the Jhansi State. Before the takeover, it was under the Peshwas from 1728 to 1804.

topPlaces.name: Khatikyana (Jhansi)
topPlaces.description: Khatikyana is a village in Jhansi district of Uttar Pradesh state, India. Popular in India for Khatikyane ki Kaali Maata.

topPlaces.name: Jhansi
topPlaces.description: Jhansi (pronounced [d͡ʑʱãː.siː] ) is a historic city in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. (Toshan) Balwant Nagar was the old name of Jhansi.

topPlaces.name: Major Dhyan Chand Hockey Stadium, Jhansi
topPlaces.description: Major Dhyan Chand Hockey Stadium is a hockey ground in Jhansi, India. The stadium is mostly used for Hockey purposes and has got coaching facility.

image.alt: Major Dhyan Chand Hockey Stadium, Jhansi

topPlaces.name: Bundelkhand
topPlaces.description: Bundelkhand (, Hindi: [bʊn.d̪eːl.kʰəɳɖ]) is a geographical and cultural region and a proposed state and also a mountain range in central and North India. It corresponds to the Post-Vedic Chedi kingdom.

topPlaces.name: Government Polytechnic Jhansi
topPlaces.description: The Government Polytechnic Jhansi is a college affiliated with the Uttar Pradesh Board of Technical Education, and approved by All India Council for Technical Education. Its principal is Naveen kumar, and it is situated on Gwalior road near the Kanpur barrier.

topPlaces.name: Electric Loco Shed, Jhansi
topPlaces.description: Electric Loco Shed, Jhansi is a motive power depot performing locomotive maintenance and repair facility for electric locomotives of the Indian Railways, located at Jhansi of the North Central Railway zone in Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Jhansi Cantonment Cemetery
topPlaces.description: Jhansi Cantonment Cemetery is a historic European cemetery situated at the military cantonment in Jhansi, Uttar Pradesh, India. A number of British civilian and military personnel who died in the Jhansi Mutiny massacre of 1857 were buried there.

items.note: Jhansi was an independent princely state ruled by the Maratha Newalkar dynasty under suzerainty of British India from 1804 till 1853, when the British authorities took over the state under the terms of the Doctrine of Lapse, and renamed it the Jhansi State. Before the takeover, it was under the Peshwas from 1728 to 1804.
Khatikyana is a village in Jhansi district of Uttar Pradesh state, India. Popular in India for Khatikyane ki Kaali Maata.

Bundelkhand (, Hindi: [bʊn.d̪eːl.kʰəɳɖ]) is a geographical and cultural region and a proposed state and also a mountain range in central and North India. It corresponds to the Post-Vedic Chedi kingdom.

The Government Polytechnic Jhansi is a college affiliated with the Uttar Pradesh Board of Technical Education, and approved by All India Council for Technical Education. Its principal is Naveen kumar, and it is situated on Gwalior road near the Kanpur barrier.

items.note: Electric Loco Shed, Jhansi is a motive power depot performing locomotive maintenance and repair facility for electric locomotives of the Indian Railways, located at Jhansi of the North Central Railway zone in Uttar Pradesh, India.

Jhansi Cantonment Cemetery is a historic European cemetery situated at the military cantonment in Jhansi, Uttar Pradesh, India. A number of British civilian and military personnel who died in the Jhansi Mutiny massacre of 1857 were buried there.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jhansi Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jhansi Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jhansi Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gwalior Airport (99 km) and the nearest railway station is Jhansi Junction (1 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jhansi Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jhansi Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jhansi Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Jhansi Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jhansi Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jhansi State, Khatikyana (Jhansi), Jhansi, Major Dhyan Chand Hockey Stadium, Jhansi, Bundelkhand.
